arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The frames are from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with Build ID `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames only show PC offsets but no function names.

To symbolicate these, I would need the debug symbols for this specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so`. This requires:

1. The matching debug symbols file (`.so` with debug info, or a separate `.debug` file)
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or `ndk-stack` to resolve the addresses


3. Microsoft's symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols`, which hosts Mono runtime debug symbols and could potentially provide the symbols for this Build ID

I don't currently have access to the original `.so` file with debug symbols, the Android NDK tools, or a way to download from the symbol server directly, so I'm limited in what I can do to resolve these addresses myself. I should first check what debugging tools are available on the system before attempting to download and process the symbols.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
I have `addr2line` available. Now I need to get the debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with Build ID `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.

Let me try to download from Microsoft's symbol server. The format for ELF debug info is:
`https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/_.debug`

Or for the full library:
`https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-<buildid>/libmonosgen-2.0.so`


I'll attempt both URLs to see which one has the symbols available.

arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "BUILDID=\"1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be\"\n# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF debug symbols format\ncurl -sSL -o /tmp/libmonosgen-debug.so \"https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so\" -w \"\\nHTTP: %{http_code}\\nSize: %{size_download}\\n\" 2>&1 | tail -5", "description": "Download debug symbols from MS symbol server", "initial_wait": 15}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "BUILDID=\"1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be\"\n# Try alternate format with sym prefix\ncurl -sSL -o /tmp/libmonosgen-debug2.so \"https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so\" -w \"\\nHTTP: %{http_code}\\nSize: %{size_download}\\n\" 2>&1 | tail -5", "description": "Try alternate symbol server format", "initial_wait": 15}

toolCalls.name: bash
